extraction.one_line_summary: DENNIS Rohan won the opening time trial in 9:39, beating KWIATKOWSKI Michał by 6 seconds after enhancing his advantage throughout the course.
tactical_patterns: DENNIS Rohan increased time gaps progressively through the course rather than fading, showing superior pacing execution.
KWIATKOWSKI Michał's Tour de France workload didn't produce expected time trial fatigue, taking second despite domestique duties.
CAMPENAERTS Victor nearly matched KWIATKOWSKI within split seconds despite European TT champion status suggesting course didn't suit pure power.

rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: Enhanced position at every split rather than holding early advantage, revealing superior race execution strategy.
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: Illness during stage severely compromised result, finishing far below expected GC contender position.
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: positive: Only rider under 10 minutes initially, showing Tour fatigue concerns were overblown for short TT efforts.
rider_signals.CAMPENAERTS Victor: neutral: European TT champion finished within seconds of podium but couldn't convert specialist status to victory.
rider_signals.OLIVEIRA Nelson: positive: First to break 10-minute barrier, indicating early start advantaged riders who could gauge pacing correctly.
rider_signals.VAN BAARLE Dylan: positive: Held provisional lead as only sub-10-minute rider initially, showing Quick-Step TT depth beyond specialists.
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: Performed very well among GC contenders despite age and non-TT specialist status.
rider_signals.CASTROVIEJO Jonathan: positive: Top-ten finish confirms elite domestique TT ability translates to individual stage racing opportunities.
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: positive: Top-ten placement signals strong condition entering three-week race for GC ambitions.
